nodes.name: 次生林
nodes.summary: 次生林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联78种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Sickle-winged guan、黑額鳴冠雉等78种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 马达加斯加
nodes.summary: 马达加斯加是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联21种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、厚嘴棉鳧、馬島潛鴨等21种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: Madagascar flufftail
nodes.summary: Madagascar flufftail（Madagascar flufftail，学名 Sarothrura insularis），分类上属于鹤形目，并归入侏秧鸡科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于马达加斯加、森林、次生林，常见栖息地包括灌丛、稀树草原。

nodes.name: 侏秧鸡科
nodes.summary: 侏秧鸡科（Sarothruridae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联7种鸟类，如Buff-spotted flufftail、Chestnut-headed flufftail、Streaky-breasted flufftail等7种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鹤形目
nodes.summary: 鹤形目（Gruiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联126种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、Grey-winged trumpeter等126种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 森林
nodes.summary: 森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联42种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan等42种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date=2020 |title=Madagascar Flufftail (Sarothrura insularis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/madflu1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.madflu1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }} Madagascar flufftails are found in Madagascar however, they are limited in the wet areas in the north and lacking from Lake Sahaka in the northeast. Its natural habitat is subtropical, tropical moist lowland forests, shrubland, grassland, and artificial areas. This is where they find their food which consists of insects and seeds. Madagascar Flufftails have a stable population of around 6,700-67,000

Its natural habitat is subtropical, tropical moist lowland forests, shrubland, grassland, and artificial areas. This is where they find their food which consists of insects and seeds. links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It has a preference for grasslands, frequently inhabiting the edges and clearings of both undisturbed and degraded forest areas. Additionally, it readily adapts to secondary bush environments, characterized by the presence of large ferns and thick grasses. Although not documented in cassava fields, it has been observed in regions of intense cultivation
